title: Single adjacent-corridor prototype
summary: Prototype one richer adjacent-pair corridor read without rewriting the whole continuous chain.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: One adjacent pair feels more continuous in place, state, and route use; no chain-wide travel rewrite begins.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Protect corridor threshold accounting, biome ownership, worldStep, and save behavior for the chosen adjacent pair.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Author one threshold note/prompt that teaches the gradient rather than naming a new biome.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Build the physical corridor proof for the chosen adjacent pair with visible carriers and a recoverable path.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Let one existing route recognize the corridor cue as optional evidence without adding another route framework.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

recommended_scope: Add one focused runtime-smoke case for treeline-tundra-corridor accounting.
Prove threshold owner changes between treeline / lichen-fell and tundra / wind-bluff without changing worldStep, lastBiomeId, or either biome visit count.
Prove a full far-edge exit into tundra advances worldStep by exactly one, increments only biomeVisits.tundra, clears corridor state, and updates lastBiomeId to tundra.
why_this_slice: Existing tests already protect the first beach/scrub corridor and full chain traversal.
Packet 142's chosen continuity seam is treeline-to-tundra after the lane-2 prompt pass, so lane 1 should isolate the high-country accounting risk before lane 3/4 deepen physical or route use.
The proof can stay test-first and likely test-only; no new corridor system is needed unless the regression fails.
preserve: src/engine/corridor.ts definitions and threshold ownership unless the new test reveals a systems bug
src/content/world-map.ts corridor-door positions and map nodes
src/engine/observation-prompts.ts and lane-2 threshold prompt copy
Route v2 definitions and support behavior
station layout, season board copy, High Pass copy, and save schema
existing beach/scrub corridor tests and full-chain traversal test
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "treeline-tundra-corridor|full adjacent corridor chain|corridor threshold"

result: Added a focused treeline-tundra-corridor runtime-smoke accounting proof.
Confirmed threshold owner and zone changes between treeline / lichen-fell and tundra / wind-bluff do not mutate worldStep, lastBiomeId, or either biome visit count.
Confirmed a full far-edge exit into tundra clears corridor state, advances worldStep by exactly one, increments only biomeVisits.tundra, and updates lastBiomeId to tundra.
Preserved corridor definitions, world-map doors/nodes, threshold prompt copy, Route v2/support behavior, station/season/High Pass copy, save schema, and existing beach/scrub plus full-chain coverage.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "treeline-tundra-corridor|full adjacent corridor chain|corridor threshold"

result: Confirmed the new treeline-tundra-corridor runtime-smoke case covers threshold owner and zone changes without save-pacing churn.
Confirmed the full far-edge exit into tundra advances worldStep exactly once, increments only biomeVisits.tundra, clears corridor state, and updates lastBiomeId.

Confirmed the implementation stayed test-only for runtime source and did not change corridor definitions, world-map doors/nodes, prompt copy, route/support behavior, station/season/High Pass copy, save schema, or geometry.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "treeline-tundra-corridor|full adjacent corridor chain|corridor threshold"
lane_1_critic_result.coordination_note: Packet 142 still has non-lane-1 workstreams, and the workspace has unrelated dirty files, so no lane-clear commit or push was attempted.

target_seed.recommended_text: Where does shelter shrink into open ground?

rationale: The live continuity docs mark treeline-to-tundra as a strong seam with shared low-growth and open-ground carriers.
Project memory says forest-to-treeline should stay mostly silent, while treeline-to-tundra can carry one quiet prompt-linked shelter cue.
Refreshing one existing prompt seed teaches the gradient without adding a new transition biome, route, map node, or corridor state.
main_agent_scope: Update only the authored text for the existing treeline-lowest-wind seed.
Preserve the seed id, biome id, family, zone ids, weather profile, required entry ids, resolver scoring, route behavior, save behavior, world-map behavior, and corridor geometry.
Add focused observation-prompt coverage for the exact text and evidence key.

result: Updated only the existing treeline-lowest-wind prompt text to Where does shelter shrink into open ground?
Added exact prompt and evidence-key coverage for the lichen-fell ridge-wind carrier set.
Preserved seed metadata, resolver/scoring behavior, route/support/save/world-map behavior, corridor geometry, ecosystem-note metadata, and biome rosters.
Confirmed the runtime source diff only refreshed the existing treeline-lowest-wind prompt text.
Confirmed seed metadata, resolver/scoring behavior, route/support/save/world-map behavior, corridor geometry, ecosystem-note metadata, and biome rosters stayed unchanged.
Confirmed the wording is short, kid-readable, science-safe, gradient-focused, and does not name a new biome or add route objective behavior.
lane_2_critic_result.coordination_note: Lane 2 is clean for packet 142, but unrelated dirty files from other lanes and remaining packet lanes make this unsafe for a lane-clear commit or push.

lane_4_scout_refinement.focus: Let the existing Low Fell route recognize the treeline-to-tundra corridor cue as optional evidence without adding another route framework.

recommended_route_change.reason: The treeline-tundra corridor already places reindeer-lichen on the treeline-side blend, and lane 2's threshold prompt uses reindeer-lichen with moss-campion and arctic-willow to teach shelter shrinking into open ground.

main_acceptance: `treeline-low-fell` accepts `reindeer-lichen` as an optional `low-rest` clue while preserving the ordered `last-tree-shape -> low-wood -> fell-bloom -> low-rest` route shape

field-request coverage proves a `3/4 clues` Low Fell route can complete from `reindeer-lichen` when `currentBiomeId` is `treeline`, `currentZoneId` is `lichen-fell`, and the observed entity zone is a corridor blend zone such as `center-blend`
controller coverage proves `hand-lens` reads corridor `reindeer-lichen` as `Notebook fit: low rest` and may retarget to it as an ordinary notebook-fit clue
paired non-hand-lens coverage keeps `note-tabs` on the physical nearest inspectable and the progress chip instead of retargeting
clue-backed Low Fell filing identity remains canonical; if `reindeer-lichen` was used, only the clue name changes in the existing filed-tail sentence
preserve: no new route, route type, field-station page, support type, planner layer, prompt seed, corridor state, corridor geometry, save schema, world-map node, or map-marker behavior
do not edit src/engine/corridor.ts, src/engine/game.ts, src/engine/observation-prompts.ts, biome geometry/content, station layout, High Pass behavior, Tundra route behavior, or lane-2 prompt copy
Brief Bloom fell-bloom behavior stays intact: moss-campion remains the active bloom alternate and reindeer-lichen does not become an active LENS CLUE winner for Low Fell

result: Added reindeer-lichen as an optional low-rest clue for treeline-low-fell while preserving the ordered last-tree-shape -> low-wood -> fell-bloom -> low-rest route shape.
Added field-request coverage proving a 3/4 clues Low Fell route can complete from reindeer-lichen when the route context is treeline / lichen-fell and the observed entity zone is center-blend.
Added controller coverage proving hand-lens can retarget to corridor reindeer-lichen as ordinary Notebook fit: low rest while note-tabs stays on the physical nearest inspectable and 3/4 clues chip.

Preserved route identity, Brief Bloom active moss-campion behavior, corridor state and geometry, station/support surfaces, save schema, world-map behavior, High Pass behavior, Tundra route behavior, and lane-2 prompt copy.
Confirmed reindeer-lichen is limited to ordinary low-rest evidence on treeline-low-fell.
Confirmed Brief Bloom active moss-campion behavior remains unchanged and reindeer-lichen is not a Low Fell LENS CLUE winner.
Confirmed hand-lens and note-tabs behavior match the implementation report.
Confirmed no route framework, corridor state or geometry, support surface, station page, save schema, world-map behavior, High Pass behavior, Tundra route behavior, or lane-2 prompt copy drift.
